audit_items.proof_trace: SnappyInputStream::RawUncompress guards buffer allocation only when uncompressed_size > _output_buffer_size (L171), but on the very first call _output_buffer is NULL and _output_buffer_size is 0; a crafted block with uncompressed_size==0 satisfies 0>0=false, leaving _output_buffer NULL, and then passes it to snappy::RawUncompress (undefined behavior). BlockCompressionInputStream::Next() (L87-88) subsequently returns *data=NULL to its caller. LZ4InputStream correctly guards with 'if (!_output_buffer)' (L202) but Snappy lacks an equivalent null-initialization check.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker sends a network RPC (binary_rpc_request.cc:91) with CompressTypeSnappy and a 1-byte compressed block (varint 0x00, representing 0 decompressed bytes); on first invocation before any prior allocation, _output_buffer remains NULL and is passed to snappy::RawUncompress, triggering undefined behavior and returning a NULL data pointer to the protobuf parser.
